objective: Make planner/orchestrator new-code-file blocks route to a validated Worker handoff path with concrete next-action guidance while keeping planner lanes blocked by default.
scope_boundary: Add a Worker handoff routing helper/check for new implementation-file writes.
Wire hooks/code-write-gate.sh to consult the helper for blocked code-file extensions.
Keep planner/orchestrator lanes blocked unless accepted Worker handoff evidence and allowed write paths cover the target path.
Add tests for planner blocks, approved Worker handoff acceptance, missing target path, wrong issue, same-family exception handling, and actionable errors.
research_summary: Issue #448 follows the recurring new-code-file block where the policy is correct but the next action is too manual. Existing execution-scope validation already enforces accepted handoff evidence, allowed write paths, lane claims, and same-family exception requirements. This slice connects that validation to the local write hook and adds a reusable handoff routing template.

sprints.name: Worker handoff route
sprints.goal: Provide a validated route from new-code-file planner blocks to approved Worker handoff execution.
tasks: Add a Worker handoff routing helper/check that validates branch issue, lane role, execution scope, allowed write paths, accepted handoff evidence, and same-family exception requirements.
Wire the code write gate to call the helper before issuing the hard new-code-file block.
Add a Worker handoff routing template compatible with the #434 package handoff lifecycle.
Add tests for planner blocks, Worker acceptance, target path missing, wrong issue, same-family without exception, and actionable next-step text.
Record issue #448 evidence and run deterministic gates.
acceptance_criteria: New code file creation from a planning/orchestration lane remains blocked unless an issue-backed execution scope and approved Worker handoff cover the target path.
The block message identifies the missing evidence type and concrete next action.
A Worker handoff artifact/template exists and is compatible with #434 package handoff lifecycle.

Worker handoff evidence can reference issue number, structured plan, execution scope, allowed write paths, target files, Worker lane/model, validation commands, QA/gate expectations, and handoff package ref.
Tests cover planner lane creating new .sh/.py/.ts/.js files being blocked.
Tests cover approved Worker handoff acceptance for the same path.
Tests cover execution scope present but target path missing.
Tests cover Worker handoff present but wrong issue number.
Tests cover same-family Worker handoff without active exception being blocked where applicable.
No product repo code edits occur.

specialist_reviews.focus: Worker handoff route helper, hook wiring, template, and tests.
specialist_reviews.summary: Bounded Sonnet worker handoff was attempted through scripts/cli_session_supervisor.py and exited nonzero after reaching max turns with no scoped implementation edits. Codex implemented and QA'd the bounded scope under the material deviation rule.

alternate_model_review.summary: Codex QA reviewed the Worker handoff route helper, hook integration, tests, and evidence after the Sonnet worker attempt failed without edits.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement governance-only Worker handoff routing for new code-file write blocks, including helper/check, hook wiring, template, tests, issue evidence, validation, review, and closeout for issue #448.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run focused validation, Local CI Gate, closeout, PR, and automerge if green.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Planner/orchestrator lanes still block new code-file creation with actionable next-step text.
Approved Worker lane plus accepted handoff evidence allows target-path creation.
Execution-scope and handoff mismatch cases fail closed.
Local CI Gate passes before merge.

material_deviation_rules: If downstream repos need propagation, create downstream issues instead of editing them here.
If Sonnet worker tooling is unavailable or hangs, record the attempt and continue with Codex implementation plus Codex QA.
If hook diagnostics need broader silent-failure repair, defer to issue #444 or a new issue.
approved_by: operator approval: no HITL, loop remaining child issues
